body:
]> 22/07/2026 7:46 Moody Mountain Weather.

Good morning and happy Wednesday! Unfortunately our run of blue bird days are over as today is looking like its going to be a moody one with cloudy skies overhead and a chance of showers that may turn to snow, so rug up and be prepared out there.

The good news is there's plenty of terrain to explore, with 32 groomed runs on offer across the resort. However, the westerly winds are strong this morning, with gusts from 35 up to 50km/h which will have an impact on lift operations. Due to the strong winds, keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates, or download the Perisher App to stay in the loop, it's worth checking before you head out.

Best of luck to all our guests competing in the ACT and Regional NSW Interschools, with racing continuing today! To our other guests: please note racing will be taking place at Front Valley, Liechardt and Mid Station, so it might be worth starting your day exploring other resort areas.

There's plenty on this week, including further demo days, live music, Station gigs, Christmas in July and more, so check the Events page or follow Perisher's socials to stay across it all!

]]> weather-blank Kosciuszko Road: Road open to Perisher.

Guthega Road: Road open to Guthega.

Alpine Way: Road open to Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> Kosciuszko Road: Road open to Perisher.

]]> Guthega Road: Road open to Guthega.

]]> Alpine Way: Road open to Bullocks Flat, Skitube.

]]> 69.3 0 0 26 155 32 *Natural snow depth is supplemented with snowmaking in certain areas. Natural snow depth is measured at roughly 1,830m by Snowy Hydro Limited who have invested in new instrumentation across the Snowy Scheme and can now provide more frequent snow depth readings at a site close to the Spencers Creek snow course. This report reflects conditions at the date and time of this report. Conditions, lifts status and operations subsequent to this report may differ.
